title: Week 3 checkpoint
canDo: Say what I have using У меня есть.
Ask, formally, if someone has something (У вас есть...?).
Say what I don't have using У меня нет + genitive.
Talk about my family using мой/моя correctly.
Say how much/how little of something I have (много/мало/сколько + genitive).

exercises.prompt: Match the Russian to its meaning.
Put the noun into the genitive.
"I don't have money" — which is correct?

items.q: I have a phone and a bicycle.
items.answer: У меня есть телефон и велосипед.

items.q: This is my family: my brother and my sister.
items.answer: Это моя семья: мой брат и моя сестра.

items.q: How much money do you have?
We have a big family, but little money.
items.answer: У нас большая семья, но мало денег.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the right order.
